option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"/Users/ege/turkcell/turkcell-subscription-system":"{\"/subscription-service/src/main/resources/db/changelog/02_outbox.sql\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines a SQL table named `outbox_messages` with the following columns:\\n\\n- `id`: a unique identifier for each message, automatically generated using a BIGSERIAL sequence.\\n- `aggregate_id`: a VARCHAR(50) field that stores the ID of the aggregate that generated the message.\\n- `payload`: a TEXT field that stores the message payload.\\n- `topic`: a VARCHAR(100) field that stores the topic of the message.\\n- `status`: a VARCHAR(20) field that stores the status of the message, with a default value of \\u0027NEW\\u0027.\\n- `created_at`: a TIMESTAMP field that stores the time the message was created, with a default value of the current timestamp.\\n- `processed_at`: a TIMESTAMP field that stores the time the message was processed.\\n\\nAdditionally, the file creates an index on the `status` column of the `outbox_messages` table, which can improve the performance of queries that filter messages by status.\",\"/turkcell-common/src/main/java/com/turkcell/event/SubscriptionCreatedEvent.java\":\"This file defines a single class:\\n\\n`SubscriptionCreatedEvent`: A class that represents an event related to the creation of a subscription, containing information such as the subscription ID, user ID, plan ID, payment details, and the subscription price.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, but rather defines a set of fields within the `SubscriptionCreatedEvent` class to store the relevant information about the subscription creation event.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/facade/SubscriptionFacadeImpl.java\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`SubscriptionFacadeImpl`: This class is responsible for managing the subscription process, including creating a pending subscription, saving the subscription created event to the Outbox service, and returning a response DTO.\\n\\n`startSubscriptionProcess`: This function initiates the subscription process by creating a pending subscription, saving the subscription created event to the Outbox service, and returning a response DTO.\\n\\nThe file is part of the Subscription Service application and is responsible for handling the subscription creation process, including integrating with the Outbox service to ensure the subscription event is persisted to the database before being published to a message queue.\",\"/k8s/payment-service/service.yaml\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/notification-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/notificationservice/NotificationServiceApplication.java\":\"This file is the main class for a Spring Boot application called \\\"NotificationServiceApplication\\\". It does not contain any other classes or functions. The file serves as the entry point for the application, and when run, it will start the Spring Boot application.\\n\\n`NotificationServiceApplication`: This is the main class for the Spring Boot application, which is responsible for bootstrapping the application and starting the Spring context.\\n\\n`main`: This is the main method that is executed when the application is started. It calls the `run()` method of the `SpringApplication` class to start the Spring Boot application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/constants/SubscriptionStatus.java\":\"This file contains a single enum `SubscriptionStatus` that defines the possible states of a subscription. It does not contain any classes or functions.\\n\\nThe `SubscriptionStatus` enum defines the following states:\\n\\n- `PENDING_PAYMENT`: The subscription is waiting for payment.\\n- `ACTIVE`: The subscription is active and in use.\\n- `CANCELLED`: The subscription has been cancelled.\\n- `REJECTED`: The subscription has been rejected.\\n\\nThis file serves as a centralized location for defining the possible states of a subscription within the subscription service.\",\"/k8s/subscription-service/service.yaml\":\"This file is a Kubernetes Service configuration for a \\\"subscription-service\\\" application. It defines the following:\\n\\n`Service`: This Kubernetes Service exposes the \\\"subscription-service\\\" application to the cluster and the external network. It specifies the service type as \\\"NodePort\\\", which means the service will be accessible on a specific node port (30001) on any node in the cluster.\\n\\nThe service selector targets pods with the label \\\"app\\u003dsubscription-service\\\", and it forwards traffic from the service port (8017) to the target port (8017) on the selected pods.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/validation/ValidationServiceImpl.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`ValidationServiceImpl`: This class implements the `ValidationService` interface and provides validation functionality for the subscription service application.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It serves as a service implementation for the validation logic in the subscription service application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/infrastructure/SubscriptionRepository.java\":\"This file contains a single interface named `SubscriptionRepository` that extends the `JpaRepository` interface provided by Spring Data JPA.\\n\\n`SubscriptionRepository`: This interface provides a set of CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for the `Subscription` entity.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. It defines a repository interface that allows interacting with the `Subscription` entity in the application\\u0027s data layer.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/entity/OutboxMessage.java\":\"This file defines an entity class for an Outbox Message, which is used in a subscription service application.\\n\\n`OutboxMessage`: This class represents an outbox message, which is a record of a message that needs to be processed and sent to a message broker.\\n\\n`id`: The unique identifier of the outbox message.\\n`aggregateId`: The ID of the aggregate that triggered the message.\\n`payload`: The content of the message.\\n`topic`: The topic of the message.\\n`status`: The current status of the message (e.g., NEW, PROCESSED).\\n`createdAt`: The timestamp when the message was created.\\n`processedAt`: The timestamp when the message was processed.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, as it is solely focused on defining the structure and properties of the `OutboxMessage` entity.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/mapper/SubscriptionMapper.java\":\"This file contains an interface named `SubscriptionMapper` that defines two mapping functions:\\n\\n`SubscriptionMapper`: This interface defines two mapping functions to convert between different data representations related to subscriptions.\\n\\n`mapToSubscription`: This function maps a `SubscriptionCreateRequest` object to a `Subscription` entity.\\n\\n`mapToEvent`: This function maps a `Subscription` entity to a `SubscriptionCreatedEvent` object.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a centralized location for mapping between different data representations used in the subscription service application.\",\"/k8s/kafka/kafka-broker.yaml\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines a Kubernetes Service and Deployment for a Kafka broker.\\n\\nThe `Service` resource creates a headless service named `kafka-broker-svc` in the `turkcell` namespace, which exposes the Kafka broker on port 9092.\\n\\nThe `Deployment` resource creates a single replica of a Kafka broker container using the `wurstmeister/kafka` image. The container is configured with various Kafka-specific environment variables, such as the broker ID, Zookeeper connection, and listener settings.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/resources/db/changelog/01_subscriptions.sql\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines a SQL table schema for a `subscriptions` table with the following columns:\\n\\n`id`: A unique identifier for the subscription, auto-generated using a BIGSERIAL primary key.\\n`user_id`: The ID of the user associated with the subscription.\\n`plan_id`: The ID of the subscription plan.\\n`type`: The type of the subscription.\\n`status`: The current status of the subscription.\\n`correlation_id`: A unique identifier for the subscription, used for tracking purposes.\\n`start_date`: The date when the subscription started.\\n`end_date`: The date when the subscription ends.\\n`error_message`: Any error message associated with the subscription.\\n`created_at`: The timestamp when the subscription was created.\\n`updated_at`: The timestamp when the subscription was last updated.\\n\\nThe file also creates two indexes on the `subscriptions` table: one on the `user_id` column and one on the `status` column, which can improve the performance of queries that filter or sort by these columns.\",\"/k8s/payment-service/deployment.yaml\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/docker/docker-compose.yaml\":\"This file is a Docker Compose configuration that sets up two PostgreSQL databases for a Turkcell subscription and payment system. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the services and volumes required for the application.\\n\\nThe file sets up the following services:\\n\\n`subscription-db`: A PostgreSQL database for the subscription service, with the database name, user, and password specified in the environment variables.\\n\\n`payment-db`: A PostgreSQL database for the payment service, with the database name, user, and password specified in the environment variables.\\n\\nThe file also defines two volumes, `sub_db_data` and `pay_db_data`, to persist the data for the respective databases.\\n\\nFinally, the file sets up a default network named `turkcell-network` for the application.\",\"/k8s/zookeeper/zookeeper.yaml\":\"This file is a Kubernetes Deployment manifest for a Zookeeper application. It defines the deployment configuration, including the number of replicas, the container image to use, and the exposed port. There are no classes or functions defined in this file. The purpose of this file is to create a Zookeeper deployment in a Kubernetes cluster.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/schedule/OutboxWorker.java\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/turkcell-common/src/main/java/com/turkcell/asd.java\":\"The provided code snippet is a Java file that contains a single class named `asd`. Since there are no functions defined within the class, the file simply defines an empty class without any additional functionality.\\n\\n`asd`: An empty class with no defined functionality.\",\"/config-server/src/main/java/com/turkcell/configserver/ConfigServerApplication.java\":\"This file is a Spring Boot application that serves as a configuration server, allowing for centralized management of application configurations.\\n\\n`ConfigServerApplication`: This is the main class that bootstraps the Spring Boot application and enables the Config Server functionality.\\n\\n`main(String[] args)`: This is the entry point of the application, which runs the `ConfigServerApplication` class.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/SubscriptionStrategy.java\":\"This file defines an interface called `SubscriptionStrategy` that provides a contract for creating pending subscriptions based on a specific subscription type.\\n\\n`SubscriptionStrategy`: An interface that defines the contract for creating pending subscriptions based on a specific subscription type.\\n\\n`getSubscriptionType()`: A method that returns the subscription type associated with the implementation of the `SubscriptionStrategy` interface.\\n\\n`createPendingSubscription(Long userId, Long planId)`: A method that creates a pending subscription for the given user and plan.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/resources/db/changelog/03_shedlock.sql\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a SQL statement that creates a table named \\\"shedlock\\\" with the following columns:\\n\\n- `name`: a VARCHAR(64) column that serves as the primary key\\n- `lock_until`: a TIMESTAMP column that stores the time until which the lock is valid\\n- `locked_at`: a TIMESTAMP column that stores the time when the lock was acquired\\n- `locked_by`: a VARCHAR(255) column that stores the identity of the entity that acquired the lock\\n\\nThe purpose of this table is to provide a way to implement distributed locking, which is a mechanism to ensure that only one process or thread can access a shared resource at a time, even in a distributed system.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/SubscriptionStrategyFactory.java\":\"This file appears to be a Spring component that manages subscription strategies based on different subscription types.\\n\\n`SubscriptionStrategyFactory`: A class that provides a factory for creating subscription strategies based on the subscription type.\\n\\n`getStrategy(SubscriptionType type)`: A function that retrieves the appropriate subscription strategy for the given subscription type.\",\"/k8s/config-server/service.yaml\":\"This file is a Kubernetes Service configuration for a \\\"config-server\\\" application. It defines the following:\\n\\n`Service`: This Kubernetes Service provides a network abstraction for the \\\"config-server\\\" application, allowing other services to connect to it using the specified name and port.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a declarative configuration that specifies the necessary details for the Kubernetes Service, such as the name, namespace, selector, ports, and service type.\",\"/turkcell-gateway-service/src/main/java/com/thy/gatewayservice/ThyGatewayServiceApplication.java\":\"This file is a Spring Boot application that serves as a gateway service for a microservices architecture. It uses the Spring Cloud Discovery Client to enable service discovery.\\n\\n`ThyGatewayServiceApplication`: This is the main class that bootstraps the Spring Boot application.\\n\\n`main(String[] args)`: This is the entry point of the application, which runs the Spring Boot application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/infrastructure/OutboxRepository.java\":\"This file contains a single interface named `OutboxRepository` that extends the `JpaRepository` interface provided by Spring Data JPA.\\n\\n`OutboxRepository`: This interface provides a way to interact with the `OutboxMessage` entity in the database using the Spring Data JPA repository pattern.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to define a repository interface for the `OutboxMessage` entity, which is likely used to manage the outbox pattern in the subscription service application.\",\"/k8s/postgres/postgres-storage.yaml\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It defines two Kubernetes PersistentVolumeClaim resources, one for a \\\"subscription-db\\\" and another for a \\\"payment-db\\\", both with a storage request of 1 Gigabyte and access mode of ReadWriteOnce. This file is used to create persistent storage volumes for the specified databases in the \\\"turkcell\\\" namespace.\",\"/payment-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/paymentservice/PaymentServiceApplication.java\":\"This file is the main class for a Spring Boot application called \\\"PaymentServiceApplication\\\". It does not contain any other classes or functions. The file serves as the entry point for the application, and when run, it will start the Spring Boot application.\\n\\n`PaymentServiceApplication`: This is the main class for the Spring Boot application, which is annotated with `@SpringBootApplication` to enable auto-configuration and component scanning.\\n\\n`main`: This is the main method that is executed when the application is started. It calls the `run` method of the `SpringApplication` class, which starts the Spring Boot application.\",\"/k8s/postgres/postgres-services.yaml\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It defines two Kubernetes services, one for a subscription database and one for a payment database, within the `turkcell` namespace. The services expose the databases on port 5432 and use the `app` labels `subscription-db` and `payment-db` to select the corresponding database pods.\",\"/turkcell-common/src/main/java/com/turkcell/dfg.java\":\"The provided code snippet does not contain any classes or functions. The file appears to be a Java source file that defines a package named `com.turkcell` and contains an empty class named `dfg`. Without any additional context, it is difficult to provide a meaningful summary of what this file does. The file seems to be a placeholder or a starting point for a larger project, but it does not provide any functionality on its own.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/strategies/GncSubscriptionStrategy.java\":\"This file contains a single class and three functions:\\n\\n`GncSubscriptionStrategy`: This class implements the `SubscriptionStrategy` interface and provides a strategy for handling GNC (Güncel) subscriptions.\\n\\n`getSubscriptionType()`: This function returns the subscription type as `SubscriptionType.GNC`.\\n\\n`createPendingSubscription(Long userId, Long planId)`: This function creates a new pending subscription with the given user ID and plan ID, sets the subscription type to `SubscriptionType.GNC`, and the status to `SubscriptionStatus.PENDING_PAYMENT`, and then saves the subscription to the repository.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a specific implementation of the `SubscriptionStrategy` interface for handling GNC subscriptions, including the creation of new pending subscriptions.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/constants/SubscriptionType.java\":\"This file contains a single enum `SubscriptionType` that defines the different types of subscriptions available in the application.\\n\\n`SubscriptionType`: An enum that defines the different types of subscriptions, including \\\"GNC\\\" and \\\"PLATINUM\\\".\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/constants/OutboxStatus.java\":\"This file contains an enumeration `OutboxStatus` that represents the different states of an outbox in a subscription service.\\n\\n`OutboxStatus`: An enumeration that represents the different states of an outbox, including `NEW`, `PROCESSED`, and `FAILED`.\",\"/k8s/notification-service/service.yaml\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document with a `\\u003cdocument\\u003e` tag, but no content within it.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/facade/SubscriptionFacade.java\":\"This file contains an interface named `SubscriptionFacade` that defines a single function:\\n\\n`SubscriptionFacade`: An interface that provides a facade for the subscription service, abstracting the underlying implementation details.\\n\\n`startSubscriptionProcess`: A function that initiates the subscription process by taking a `SubscriptionCreateRequest` and returning a `SubscriptionResponseDTO`.\\n\\nThis file is part of the subscription service application and provides a high-level interface for managing the subscription process.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/dto/SubscriptionCreateRequest.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `SubscriptionCreateRequest`, which is a data transfer object (DTO) used for creating a new subscription. The class has several fields that represent the necessary information for creating a subscription, such as the user ID, plan ID, subscription type, and credit card details. The class uses Lombok annotations to generate boilerplate code, and it also includes validation constraints using the Jakarta Validation API.\\n\\n`SubscriptionCreateRequest`: A DTO class that represents the necessary information for creating a new subscription, including user ID, plan ID, subscription type, and credit card details.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/util/SubscriptionStrategyResolver.java\":\"This file appears to contain a single class, `SubscriptionStrategyResolver`, but it does not have any functions defined within it. The file seems to be a utility class that is responsible for resolving the appropriate subscription strategy, but without any further context or implementation details, it\\u0027s difficult to provide a more detailed summary.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/outbox/OutboxService.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes, but it does contain the following function:\\n\\n`OutboxService`: An interface that defines a service for saving subscription creation events to an outbox.\\n\\n`saveSubscriptionCreatedEvent`: A function that saves a subscription creation event to the outbox, taking a `Subscription` entity and a `SubscriptionCreateRequest` as parameters.\\n\\nThis file is part of a subscription service application and provides an interface for saving subscription creation events to an outbox, which is a common pattern in event-driven architectures.\",\"/k8s/subscription-service/deployment.yaml\":\"This file is a Kubernetes Deployment configuration for a service called \\\"subscription-service\\\" in the \\\"turkcell\\\" namespace. It specifies the deployment\\u0027s configuration, including the number of replicas, the container image to use, and the readiness and liveness probes for the service.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines the following Kubernetes resources:\\n\\n`Deployment`: Defines a deployment for the \\\"subscription-service\\\" application, with 2 replicas and the specified container image.\\n`Selector`: Defines the labels used to select the pods that belong to this deployment.\\n`Template`: Defines the pod template, including the container specification and the readiness and liveness probes.\\n`Containers`: Defines the container for the \\\"subscription-service\\\" application, including the image, ports, and probes.\\n`ReadinessProbe`: Defines the readiness probe for the container, which checks the \\\"/actuator/health/readiness\\\" endpoint on port 8017.\\n`LivenessProbe`: Defines the liveness probe for the container, which checks the \\\"/actuator/health/liveness\\\" endpoint on port 8017.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/subscription/strategies/PlatinumSubscriptionStrategy.java\":\"This file contains a single class and three functions:\\n\\n`PlatinumSubscriptionStrategy`: This class implements the `SubscriptionStrategy` interface and provides the implementation for the platinum subscription type.\\n\\n`getSubscriptionType()`: This function returns the subscription type as `SubscriptionType.PLATINUM`.\\n\\n`createPendingSubscription(Long userId, Long planId)`: This function creates a new pending subscription with the platinum subscription type and saves it to the repository.\\n\\nThe file is part of the subscription service application and provides the implementation for the platinum subscription strategy.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/config/SchedulerConfig.java\":\"This file is a Spring configuration class that sets up scheduling and distributed locking for a subscription service application.\\n\\n`SchedulerConfig`: This class configures the scheduling and distributed locking for the application.\\n\\n`lockProvider`: This function creates a `LockProvider` bean that uses a JDBC-based lock provider to ensure that scheduled tasks are executed only once across multiple instances of the application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/entity/Subscription.java\":\"This file defines a `Subscription` entity class for a subscription service. It represents a subscription record in the database, with various fields such as user ID, plan ID, subscription type, status, correlation ID, start and end dates, error message, and timestamps for creation and update.\\n\\n`Subscription`: Represents a subscription record in the database, with fields for user ID, plan ID, subscription type and status, correlation ID, start and end dates, error message, and timestamps.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, as it is a data model class used for persistence in a subscription service application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/domain/dto/SubscriptionResponseDTO.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`SubscriptionResponseDTO`: A data transfer object (DTO) that represents the response for a subscription operation, including the subscription ID, correlation ID, status, message, and creation timestamp.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It is a simple DTO class used to encapsulate the response data for a subscription-related operation.\",\"/turkcell-common/src/main/java/com/turkcell/event/BaseEvent.java\":\"This file defines an abstract `BaseEvent` class that serves as a base class for event-related entities in a software application.\\n\\n`BaseEvent`: An abstract class that provides a common structure and functionality for event-related entities, including a unique event ID, correlation ID, creation timestamp, and version information.\\n\\n`eventId`: Generates a unique UUID for the event.\\n\\n`correlationId`: Stores a correlation ID for the event.\\n\\n`createdAt`: Stores the timestamp when the event was created.\\n\\n`version`: Stores the version of the event schema.\",\"/k8s/config-server/deployment.yaml\":\"This file is a Kubernetes Deployment configuration for a Spring Cloud Config Server. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the deployment specifications for the Config Server.\\n\\nThe key elements of the configuration are:\\n\\n`Deployment`: Defines the deployment of the Config Server, including the number of replicas, the container image, and the environment variables.\\n`metadata`: Provides metadata about the deployment, such as the name, namespace, and labels.\\n`spec`: Specifies the desired state of the deployment, including the selector, template, and container configuration.\\n`containers`: Defines the container for the Config Server, including the image, ports, and environment variables.\\n`readinessProbe`: Configures the readiness probe for the Config Server, which checks the health of the application.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to deploy a Spring Cloud Config Server that can be used to manage the configuration of other microservices in the Turkcell application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/SubscriptionServiceApplication.java\":\"This file is the main class for a Spring Boot application called \\\"SubscriptionServiceApplication\\\". It does not contain any other classes or functions. The main purpose of this file is to bootstrap the Spring Boot application and start the application\\u0027s main method.\\n\\n`SubscriptionServiceApplication`: This is the main class that runs the Spring Boot application.\\n\\n`main(String[] args)`: This is the main method that starts the Spring Boot application.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/validation/ValidationService.java\":\"This file defines an interface named `ValidationService` without any classes or functions. The purpose of this interface is to provide a contract for a service that performs validation operations, but the specific details of the validation logic are not provided in this file.\",\"/k8s/notification-service/deployment.yaml\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/subscription-service/src/main/java/com/turkcell/subscriptionservice/application/outbox/OutboxServiceImpl.java\":\"This file appears to be a Spring Service implementation for handling the Outbox pattern in a subscription service application.\\n\\n`OutboxServiceImpl`: A service implementation that handles the persistence and publication of subscription-related events using the Outbox pattern.\\n\\n`saveSubscriptionCreatedEvent`: A function that creates a `SubscriptionCreatedEvent` object, serializes it to JSON, and saves it as an `OutboxMessage` in the repository.\",\"/k8s/postgres/postgres-deployments.yaml\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines two Kubernetes Deployments for two separate PostgreSQL databases:\\n\\n`Deployment`: This Deployment creates a PostgreSQL database for the \\\"subscription\\\" service, with a single replica, and mounts a persistent volume claim for the database data.\\n\\n`Deployment`: This Deployment creates a PostgreSQL database for the \\\"payment\\\" service, with a single replica, and mounts a persistent volume claim for the database data.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide the necessary Kubernetes configuration to deploy and manage the PostgreSQL databases for the \\\"subscription\\\" and \\\"payment\\\" services within the \\\"turkcell\\\" namespace.\",\"/k8s/namespace.yaml\":\"This file defines a Kubernetes namespace named \\\"turkcell\\\". It does not contain any classes or functions. This file is used to create a new namespace in a Kubernetes cluster, which can be used to organize and manage resources within the cluster.\"}"}

option.name: fileSkeletonMaps
option.value:
